task_completion.description: Open the EIR specification document on the desktop to identify the required property set and properties.
task_completion.visual_evidence: A text editor or document viewer open showing the contents of 'EIR_asset_requirements.txt'.

task_completion.description: Create the custom property set in Bonsai.
task_completion.visual_evidence: The Bonsai (BlenderBIM) interface showing the Custom Property Sets or IFC Project setup panel with 'Pset_ClientAssetData' added as a new property set template.

task_completion.description: Add the required properties to the custom property set template.
task_completion.visual_evidence: The Bonsai interface showing the properties 'AssetID', 'ConditionScore', and 'IsMaintainable' configured within the 'Pset_ClientAssetData' template.

task_completion.description: Apply the custom property set to door and/or window elements in the model.
task_completion.visual_evidence: The Object Properties panel in Bonsai showing 'Pset_ClientAssetData' attached to a selected IfcDoor or IfcWindow element, with the 3D viewport or Outliner showing the element selected.

task_completion.description: Save the enriched IFC project to the specified path.
task_completion.visual_evidence: A save dialog in Blender/Bonsai or a file browser/terminal showing the file 'fzk_eir_compliant.ifc' successfully saved in the '/home/ga/BIMProjects/' directory.

integrity.description: The agent used the Bonsai (BlenderBIM) graphical interface to create and apply the property sets.
integrity.visual_evidence: Screenshots showing interaction with the Blender/Bonsai UI panels (e.g., Scene Properties, Object Properties) rather than executing a Python script in the terminal to bypass the UI.

integrity.description: The agent selected actual 3D elements (doors/windows) in the viewport or outliner to apply the properties.
integrity.visual_evidence: The Blender 3D viewport or Outliner shows doors or windows actively selected (highlighted) when properties are being applied.

integrity.description: The agent did not manually type the property set definitions into the raw IFC file using a text editor.
integrity.visual_evidence: Absence of a text editor (like nano, vim, gedit) open with raw IFC STEP physical file syntax being manually edited to insert the property sets.

privileged_info_for_vlm: The required custom property set name is 'Pset_ClientAssetData'. The required properties to be added are 'AssetID', 'ConditionScore', and 'IsMaintainable'. The target elements are IfcDoor and IfcWindow (the model contains 5 doors and 11 windows). The property set must be applied to at least 8 of these elements. The final output file must be saved exactly to '/home/ga/BIMProjects/fzk_eir_compliant.ifc'.
